commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Wait for contributor-supplied real behavior proof; automation should not take over because the code/test patch is already present and the missing evidence must come from a real setup.; Cleared: The diff only changes a diagnostic docs URL literal and its test expectation, with no dependency, permission, secret, network, or execution-surface changes. (sha=9fa01c69834a21e333bad806447e8c3dddaaafe2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the gateway untrusted plugin fallback override error and its colocated test expectation to link to the gateway configuration reference plugins anchor.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main emits the old `tools/plugin#runtime-helpers` URL, the plugin tools page has no matching heading, and the configuration reference documents the requested key under `#plugins`. I did not execute the runtime path because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Wait for contributor-supplied real behavior proof; automation should not take over because the code/test patch is already present and the missing evidence must come from a real setup.

target.title: fix(gateway): point allowModelOverride error to a valid docs anchor
target.body:
The error raised when a plugin requests a fallback provider/model override without 'plugins.entries.<id>.subagent.allowModelOverride' linked to 'docs.openclaw.ai/tools/plugin#runtime-helpers'. That page has no 'runtime-helpers' heading; the anchor resolves to nothing.

Point users instead at 'gateway/configuration-reference#plugins', which is the canonical config-reference section and where 'plugins.entries.<id>.subagent.allowModelOverride' is documented (see docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md around the 'Plugins' heading). The error already tells the user the exact key to search for, so they can ctrl-F on that section.

Refs #65860

## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

- Problem:
- Solution:
- What changed:
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):

Explain why this change should exist now. Link it to the user pain, failure mode, maintainer need, or product goal. If this is purely mechanical, write `N/A`.

-

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only. Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes; terminal screenshots and copied live output count. Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

After:
